family.choir: Seattle Pro Musica has wrapped its 2025-26 auditions, but Mrs. Lang can email auditions@seattlepromusica.org to join the August 2026-27 season mailing list. Rehearsals are Tuesday evenings 6:30-9:30 pm at Epiphany Parish Seattle (1805 38th Ave). Annual dues $75, scholarships available. Choir of the Sound is also recruiting for 2026-27—watch their Instagram for details.
family.toddler: Sno-Isle Libraries offers weekly Toddler Storytime (18 mo-3 yrs) at both Edmonds and Lynnwood branches. Edmonds has Baby Storytime and Preschool Storytime too; Lynnwood lists identical 9:30 am and 10:30 am Toddler sessions. All free, no registration required—just show up.

global_news.findings: Suspect charged with attempted assassination of Trump at Washington dinner: A 31-year-old California man has been charged after allegedly opening fire at the White House Correspondents' Dinner on Saturday night. The suspect, Cole Tomas Allen, reportedly wanted to kill as many high-level officials as possible. The incident has reignited concerns about political violence in America and raised questions about security protocols at high-profile events.

King Charles III arrives for U.S. state visit amid UK-US tensions over Iran: King Charles III has begun a four-day state visit to Washington, where he is expected to address Congress and meet with President Trump. The visit comes at a delicate time as the UK and US navigate differences over the ongoing war with Iran. The King is expected to express sympathy over the recent assassination attempt while also seeking to strengthen the transatlantic relationship.

Germany commits to massive military expansion as NATO marks 70th anniversary: German President Frank-Walter Steinmeier announced that Germany will become the backbone of conventional defense in Europe, pledging 500 billion euros ($569 billion) for military infrastructure. This represents a historic shift for Germany, traditionally cautious about military spending, as concerns grow about US commitment to European defense under Trump. Steinmeier stated a well-armed Germany is now less threatening to Europe than a poorly armed one.

Iran offers conditional reopening of Strait of Hormuz: Iranian officials have indicated they would consider reopening the vital Strait of Hormuz to commercial shipping if the US lifts its naval blockade and ends the war. This development comes as global oil prices remain elevated above $100/barrel due to the ongoing conflict. The offer represents a potential diplomatic opening, though US officials have not yet responded publicly.

Ceasefire at risk as Pakistan and Afghanistan report cross-border attacks: Tensions are escalating between Pakistan and Afghanistan with both sides reporting attacks across their shared border. The violence threatens to derail recent peace talks and comes as UNICEF warns Afghanistan could lose up to 25,000 children due to the ongoing humanitarian crisis. The situation remains volatile with both countries accusing each other of harboring militants.

UK to deny asylum protections to foreign sex offenders: The British government announced it will amend its Border Security, Asylum and Immigration Bill to exclude foreign nationals with sex offense convictions from receiving refugee protections. Home Secretary Yvette Cooper stated that sex offenders who pose risks to communities should not benefit from asylum protections. The move is part of broader efforts to strengthen border security and speed up asylum decisions.

intellectual_journals.findings: Flora Champy explores Marcel Proust's 1905 essay 'On Reading' as a counter-argument to John Ruskin's moral utilitarian view of literature. Proust insists books are not instruments of moral betterment but portals to 'personal freedom'—they preserve vivid memories of people and places long gone, making time graspable without abolishing it. Reading, for Proust, is a unique, non-instrumental form of communication that connects us to 'the most powerful and smartest minds in the world,' distinct from the social constraints of everyday speech. The essay frames literature as a technology of memory and [TRUNCATED]

A Jacobin analysis warns that routine delegation of judgment to chatbots risks a quiet erosion of human critical capacity—what the author terms 'cognitive debt.' Citing a new MIT study showing reduced brain activity in habitual chatbot users, the piece argues that outsourcing thought to LLMs undermines the Enlightenment ideal of thinking for oneself (Kant’s ‘What Is Enlightenment?’). Far from apocalyptic AI takeover, the danger is a ‘decaffeinated subjectivity’ that weakens democratic participation and emancipatory politics. Efficiency gains, the essay contends, may trade away the very cogniti [TRUNCATED]

Will Alden reports from a Los Angeles ‘Tehrangeles’ picnic where exiled supporters of Reza Pahlavi celebrated Easter Sunday as a rehearsal for ‘Day Zero’—the anticipated collapse of the Islamic Republic. The Constitutionalist Party of Iran, founded in 1994 by a royal minister in exile, stages weekly rallies outside the Wilshire Federal Building, blending Persian pop anthems with pro-Trump signage. Alden captures the generational split: older monarchists nostalgic for the Shah, younger hardliners who want restoration ‘by any means necessary,’ and liberals invoking democratic values. The piece s [TRUNCATED]

Francine Prose reviews two new novels—Lee Clay Johnson’s ‘Bloodline’ and Carl Hiaasen’s ‘Fever Beach’—that tackle America’s present crisis through dark satire. Johnson’s protagonist Winston Alcorn amputates his own hand in an insurance scam to reclaim what he believes is his Confederate birthright, literalizing the South’s ‘blood in the game’ mythology. Hiaasen’s Florida noir, meanwhile, savages megamansion developers and toxic-waste profiteers. Both novels, Prose argues, convert rage at environmental destruction, historical amnesia, and class resentment into propulsive fiction without sacrifi [TRUNCATED]

**Omi** (San Francisco) leads the pack with a $2.7M-funded pendant that captures daily conversations and turns them into structured memory. Used by Salesforce and Tony R [TRUNCATED]

**MagicSchool AI** (Denver) dominates with $62.4M in funding and 7M+ educators using their platform. Offers comprehensive AI support for lesson planning, assessments, IEPs, and communication. Recent updates include Class Writing Feedback aligned to teacher rubrics and Ma [TRUNCATED]

The wearable AI device market has matured significantly with focus on lifelogging and productivity applications. Key developments include:

**Omi** leads with comprehensive conversation capture and memory structuring, trusted by major enterprises and offering extensive third-party integrations through their open platform.

**Fieldy** focuses on effortless note-taking with speaker recognition capab [TRUNCATED]

triadic_ontology.findings: The Triadic Closure of Existence: Co-Emergence of Binding, Transformation, and Relational Readout (Zenodo, 15 Apr 2026) argues that stable reality emerges only when three functional roles—binding, transformation, and relational readout—phase-lock simultaneously, replacing the additive “forces + particles” model with a non-negotiable triadic closure. A Triadic Ontology with SU(3) Invariants (Trent Slade, Zenodo 2025) operationalises the idea: the qutrit becomes the ontic primitive, SU(3) symmetry supplies eight interaction generators, and a discrete ℤ₃ rotation operator enforces closure without collapse, yielding an executable 3–8–2 schema ready for quantum-network simulations. Consciousness Between Fact and Value: A Triadic Neurophenomenology (Kenneth Hammat, PhilPapers 2025) maps the same triad onto mind: object (material), idea (mental), relation (axiological) are irreducible domains unified by consciousness; recursive coupling between idea and relation is proposed as the origin of r [TRUNCATED]

ai_systems.findings: The autonomous research landscape has matured rapidly in 2026. Google Cloud AI Research introduced PaperOrchestra, a five-agent system that transforms raw experimental logs into submission-ready LaTeX manuscripts, executing ~4,000 LLM calls per paper to generate literature reviews, figures, and verified citations. Parallel work from Cambridge and Berkeley shows semi-autonomous astrophysics agents (Cmbagent/Denario) now outperform human experts in cosmological inference challenges when given strategic human guidance. Framework standardization is advancing through the Auton Agentic AI Framework (arXiv:2602.23720), which separates cognitive blueprints from runtime engines using a Model Context Protocol for cross-language portability and formal auditability. Prompt engineering has shifted from eliciting reasoning to directing it: 2026's frontier models (o3, Claude 4.6, Gemini 2.5 Pro) perform internal chain-of-thought before responding, requiring new techniques like "ask clarifying questio [TRUNCATED]

uap.findings: Congress is accelerating UAP disclosure through multiple channels. The House Oversight Committee's Task Force on Declassification, led by Rep. Anna Paulina Luna, continues investigating federal transparency failures and has formally requested AARO video files of UAP sightings. Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth confirmed the Pentagon will "be in full compliance" with Trump's disclosure directive, as AARO's caseload exceeds 2,000 incidents. In classified SCIF briefings, senators including Marco Rubio have received testimony about recovered non-human craft and bodies, while Rep. Eric Burlison confirmed a March 25 bipartisan briefing occurred amid the mysterious disappearance of retired Air Force General Neil McCasland, a former Wright-Patterson commander with knowledge of classified aerospace programs. The FY26 NDAA now mandates Pentagon briefings on UAP intercepts since 2004, and scientists like Dr. Eric Davis claim they've already briefed Congress on crash retrieval programs and Non-Human  [TRUNCATED]

enriched_articles.title: The role of literature as the key to personal freedom
enriched_articles.text:
# Does reading do us any good?

## Stripped of easy moralising, literature makes us relish the search for truth in an age when many believe truth to be dead

by Flora Champy + BIO

Does reading turn us into better people? Does it make us more sensitive and empathetic? Does it improve our judgment? And if it is not edifying, then what good does it do? About 120 years ago, the indecisive, dilettante offspring of a wealthy physician found these questions so important that he took up his pen to argue that, no, books are never the instruments of moral betterment. He based his argument on his own memories. Though he had always been an avid reader, books, he claimed, never gave him any sort of useful, respectable instruction. That does not mean they were meaningless – far from it; they shaped his apprehension of life by preserving indelible impressions of his surroundings. Relatives now long gone, places he hadn’t seen in years – these were nevertheless still present in his mind through memories of his readings. Books helped keep past sensations alive. Literature made time tangible: something to be grasped without being abolished.

enriched_articles.title: Movements Need the Critical Thinking That AI Destroys
enriched_articles.text:
# Jacobin

## Movements Need the Critical Thinking That AI Destroys

Login

[Our special spring issue is out now. Get a discounted subscription to our print magazine today. Your support keeps us publishing!](https://jacobin.com/subscribe/?code=TEENJACOBIN)

# Movements Need the Critical Thinking That AI Destroys

Struggles against oppression start with people critically reflecting on their experiences. What happens to such struggles when we outsource our thinking to AI and replace human interlocutors with sycophantic chatbots?

![AI Companion App Replika Illustration Photos](https://media.jacobin.com/images/2026/4/289547300662.jpg)

With the emergence of chatbots, the outsourcing of thinking — and therefore also the critical questioning of existing social norms and power relations — is taking a new form. (Jaap Arriens / NurPhoto via Getty Images)

Jacobin‘s special spring issue, “Teen Jacobin,” is out now. [Follow this link to get a discounted subscription to our beautiful print quarterly.](https://jacobin.com/subscribe/?code=TEENJACOBIN)

Millions of people are now asking chatbots to summarize books, draft emails, and even explain political events to them. But what looks from one  [TRUNCATED]

enriched_articles.title: King Charles III arrives at White House for four-day US visit
enriched_articles.text: Britain’s King Charles III and Queen Camilla were greeted at the White House by US President Donald Trump and First Lady Melaia to begin a four-day state visit. The celebration comes as the United States prepares to mark the 250th anniversary of its independence from the UK.
